name: Prefect Cloud API
what_it_does: Python-native workflow orchestration as a service — wrap any Python function with @flow and @task decorators to get managed scheduling, retries, observability, and a REST API for triggering and monitoring runs.
use_cases: Triggering and monitoring data pipeline runs from AI agents via the REST API
Building resilient ETL workflows with automatic retry logic, caching, and observability
Scheduling periodic data processing jobs with cron or interval triggers
Orchestrating multi-step ML workflows (data prep, training, evaluation, deployment)
Event-driven pipelines triggered by external webhooks or state changes in other systems
not_for: Real-time streaming data processing — Prefect is for discrete flow runs, not continuous stream processors
Teams that need a DAG-centric paradigm — Prefect uses Python control flow, not explicit DAG definitions
Sub-second scheduling precision — minimum scheduling granularity is approximately 1 minute
best_when: Your agent needs to trigger, monitor, and react to the results of data pipeline runs, and the pipelines are already Python-based.
avoid_when: You need complex cross-language orchestration, a visual DAG editor as the primary authoring interface, or sub-minute scheduling.

auth.notes: Prefect Cloud API keys are generated per-workspace in the UI and set via PREFECT_API_KEY and PREFECT_API_URL environment variables. Service account API keys are available on paid plans for CI/CD and production use. No fine-grained scope control — keys have full workspace access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 3 workspaces, 5,000 task runs per month, 1 service account
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro: $29/month per workspace — unlimited task runs, 20 service accounts, RBAC, SSO. Enterprise: custom pricing with dedicated infrastructure.
pricing.notes: Free tier is functional for development and small pipelines. The 5K task run/month limit can be reached quickly with retries or high-frequency tasks. Open-source Prefect (self-hosted) has no limits — Cloud is the managed SaaS offering.

performance.rate_limits: REST API: 2,000 requests per minute per workspace on paid plans; lower on free tier. Flow run submission is not rate-limited independently but is subject to workspace limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP error codes with JSON bodies including detail fields. Task and flow run state transitions surface structured state objects with clear terminal states (COMPLETED, FAILED, CRASHED). Retry exhaustion produces descriptive failure states. SDK exceptions are typed and include flow run context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flow runs can be given explicit IDs for idempotent submission. Tasks within a flow support result caching via cache_key_fn — identical inputs return cached results within the cache expiry window.
known_agent_gotchas: Prefect worker (agent process) must be running in the execution environment — Cloud is orchestration only, not execution
Flow code must be available to the worker — deployment packaging (Docker, git storage) must be configured before triggering via API
REST API for flow run creation requires knowing the deployment ID, not just the flow name — resolve deployment first
State polling is the recommended pattern for waiting on run completion; no native webhook push on run finish without Automations
Task run cache keys are scoped to the deployment and cache expiry; changing flow code may not invalidate cache as expected

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all Cloud API endpoints. Prefect Secrets blocks provide a managed secrets store for flow credentials — secrets never leave Prefect's encrypted storage. RBAC available on Pro/Enterprise plans for team-level access control. SOC2 Type II certified. Self-hosted deployments inherit the security posture of the host infrastructure.
